'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mure-jinja}} [[JA:牟禮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11570305}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|茨木市の牟禮神社|飯綱町の牟禮神社|牟礼神社 (飯綱町)}} {{神社 |名称 = Mure Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mure-jinja torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}} Nakamura-chō 5-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|14.88|N|135|34|51.16|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mure Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mure Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mure Shrine''' (むれじんじゃ; simplified characters: '''牟礼神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} (たてはやすさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのこやねのみこと) === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' lists it as “Mure Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940}}, Settsu Province,” and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'', in the section on {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, there is the passage “Miko Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of Mure no Wake,” leading to a theory that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおなかつひこのみこと), the ancestor of the Mure clan.However, the relationship between “Mure no Wake” and this locality is not clear (from the “Mure Village” entry in ''Place Names of Osaka Prefecture''). Nevertheless, it is said that in order to avoid the temple and shrine burnings carried out by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, the shrine falsely claimed its enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247}} and the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805}}, whom Nobunaga revered, and this claim remained thereafter. In {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}), it was returned from the Mure settlement to the Nakamura settlement. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|WD=Q18639}}), due to river improvement works on the {{ill|lt=Ai River|Q11450512|WD=Q11450512}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site. In Shōwa 15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633}}), it received and repurposed the building of the north-facing Hachiman Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|WD=Q11574477}}, which had been built in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|WD=Q6747}}) by {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|WD=Q1882775}}, as its shrine hall. In Shōwa 54 ({{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|WD=Q2484}}), the main hall was restored. === Grounds === File:Mure-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall　　 File:Mure 03.JPG|Worship hall There are no other subsidiary shrines within the grounds. === On-site information === ; Address * Nakamura-chō 5-7, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Hankyu Corporation|Q473058|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|WD=Q2053737|lt=Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|WD=Q3969806}} (about a 15-minute walk) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), ''Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District'' (1974). {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, ''Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples'' (1989). {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} * Osaka Prefectural Shinto Youth Association (ed.), ''Directory of Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture'' (1971). {{NCID|BA39399841}} * ''Japanese Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Ibaraki City—entries for Nakamura and Mure Village === External links === {{Commonscat|Mure-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/ibaraki-city/m12_21_ibaragi_mure.html Mure Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto navigation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mure Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mure Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mure-jinja}} [[JA:牟禮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11570305}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|茨木市の牟禮神社|飯綱町の牟禮神社|牟礼神社 (飯綱町)}} {{神社 |名称 = Mure Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mure-jinja torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}} Nakamura-chō 5-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|14.88|N|135|34|51.16|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mure Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mure Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mure Shrine''' (むれじんじゃ; simplified characters: '''牟礼神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} (たてはやすさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのこやねのみこと) === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' lists it as “Mure Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940}}, Settsu Province,” and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}'', in the section on {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, there is the passage “Miko Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of Mure no Wake,” leading to a theory that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおなかつひこのみこと), the ancestor of the Mure clan.However, the relationship between “Mure no Wake” and this locality is not clear (from the “Mure Village” entry in ''Place Names of Osaka Prefecture''). Nevertheless, it is said that in order to avoid the temple and shrine burnings carried out by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, the shrine falsely claimed its enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247}} and the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805}}, whom Nobunaga revered, and this claim remained thereafter. In {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}), it was returned from the Mure settlement to the Nakamura settlement. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|WD=Q18639}}), due to river improvement works on the {{ill|lt=Ai River|Q11450512|WD=Q11450512}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site. In Shōwa 15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633}}), it received and repurposed the building of the north-facing Hachiman Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|WD=Q11574477}}, which had been built in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|WD=Q6747}}) by {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|WD=Q1882775}}, as its shrine hall. In Shōwa 54 ({{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|WD=Q2484}}), the main hall was restored. === Grounds === File:Mure-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall　　 File:Mure 03.JPG|Worship hall There are no other subsidiary shrines within the grounds. === On-site information === ; Address * Nakamura-chō 5-7, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Hankyu Corporation|Q473058|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|WD=Q2053737|lt=Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|WD=Q3969806}} (about a 15-minute walk) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), ''Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District'' (1974). {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, ''Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples'' (1989). {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} * Osaka Prefectural Shinto Youth Association (ed.), ''Directory of Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture'' (1971). {{NCID|BA39399841}} * ''Japanese Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Ibaraki City—entries for Nakamura and Mure Village === External links === {{Commonscat|Mure-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/ibaraki-city/m12_21_ibaragi_mure.html Mure Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto navigation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mure Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mure Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * むれじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120905000160 == Commons category (P373) == * Mure-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市中村町5-7', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mure-jinja}} [[JA:牟禮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11570305}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|茨木市の牟禮神社|飯綱町の牟禮神社|牟礼神社 (飯綱町)}} {{神社 |名称 = Mure Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mure-jinja torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Nakamura-chō 5-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|14.88|N|135|34|51.16|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mure Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mure Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mure Shrine''' (むれじんじゃ; simplified characters: '''牟礼神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (たてはやすさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのこやねのみこと) === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists it as “Mure Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}, Settsu Province,” and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'', in the section on {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, there is the passage “Miko Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of Mure no Wake,” leading to a theory that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおなかつひこのみこと), the ancestor of the Mure clan.However, the relationship between “Mure no Wake” and this locality is not clear (from the “Mure Village” entry in ''Place Names of Osaka Prefecture''). Nevertheless, it is said that in order to avoid the temple and shrine burnings carried out by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine falsely claimed its enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}, whom Nobunaga revered, and this claim remained thereafter. In {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}), it was returned from the Mure settlement to the Nakamura settlement. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|WD=Q18639|1=1939}}), due to river improvement works on the {{ill|lt=Ai River|Q11450512|WD=Q11450512|1=Ai River}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site. In Shōwa 15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}}), it received and repurposed the building of the north-facing Hachiman Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|WD=Q11574477|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}}, which had been built in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|WD=Q6747|1=1606}}) by {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|WD=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}}, as its shrine hall. In Shōwa 54 ({{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|WD=Q2484|1=1979}}), the main hall was restored. === Grounds === File:Mure-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall　　 File:Mure 03.JPG|Worship hall There are no other subsidiary shrines within the grounds. === On-site information === ; Address * Nakamura-chō 5-7, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Hankyu Corporation|Q473058|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu|1=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|WD=Q2053737|lt=Kyoto Main Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|WD=Q3969806|1=Ibaraki-shi Station}} (about a 15-minute walk) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), ''Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District'' (1974). {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, ''Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples'' (1989). {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} * Osaka Prefectural Shinto Youth Association (ed.), ''Directory of Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture'' (1971). {{NCID|BA39399841}} * ''Japanese Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ibaraki City—entries for Nakamura and Mure Village === External links === {{Commonscat|Mure-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/ibaraki-city/m12_21_ibaragi_mure.html Mure Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto navigation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mure Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mure Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mure Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mure Shrine''' (むれじんじゃ; simplified characters: '''牟礼神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (たてはやすさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのこやねのみこと) === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists it as “Mure Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}, Settsu Province,” and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'', in the section on {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, there is the passage “Miko Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of Mure no Wake,” leading to a theory that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおなかつひこのみこと), the ancestor of the Mure clan.However, the relationship between “Mure no Wake” and this locality is not clear (from the “Mure Village” entry in ''Place Names of Osaka Prefecture''). Nevertheless, it is said that in order to avoid the temple and shrine burnings carried out by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine falsely claimed its enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}, whom Nobunaga revered, and this claim remained thereafter. In {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}), it was returned from the Mure settlement to the Nakamura settlement. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|WD=Q18639|1=1939}}), due to river improvement works on the {{ill|lt=Ai River|Q11450512|WD=Q11450512|1=Ai River}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site. In Shōwa 15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}}), it received and repurposed the building of the north-facing Hachiman Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|WD=Q11574477|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}}, which had been built in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|WD=Q6747|1=1606}}) by {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|WD=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}}, as its shrine hall. In Shōwa 54 ({{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|WD=Q2484|1=1979}}), the main hall was restored. === Grounds === File:Mure-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall　　 File:Mure 03.JPG|Worship hall There are no other subsidiary shrines within the grounds. === On-site information === ; Address * Nakamura-chō 5-7, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Hankyu Corporation|Q473058|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu|1=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|WD=Q2053737|lt=Kyoto Main Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|WD=Q3969806|1=Ibaraki-shi Station}} (about a 15-minute walk) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), ''Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District'' (1974). {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, ''Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples'' (1989). {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} * Osaka Prefectural Shinto Youth Association (ed.), ''Directory of Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture'' (1971). {{NCID|BA39399841}} * ''Japanese Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ibaraki City—entries for Nakamura and Mure Village === External links === {{Commonscat|Mure-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/ibaraki-city/m12_21_ibaragi_mure.html Mure Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto navigation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mure Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mure Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mure-jinja}} [[JA:牟禮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11570305}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|茨木市の牟禮神社|飯綱町の牟禮神社|牟礼神社 (飯綱町)}} {{神社 |名称 = Mure Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mure-jinja torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Nakamura-chō 5-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|14.88|N|135|34|51.16|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mure Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mure Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mure Shrine''' (むれじんじゃ; simplified characters: '''牟礼神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (たてはやすさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのこやねのみこと) === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists it as “Mure Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}, Settsu Province,” and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'', in the section on {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, there is the passage “Miko Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of Mure no Wake,” leading to a theory that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおなかつひこのみこと), the ancestor of the Mure clan.However, the relationship between “Mure no Wake” and this locality is not clear (from the “Mure Village” entry in ''Place Names of Osaka Prefecture''). Nevertheless, it is said that in order to avoid the temple and shrine burnings carried out by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine falsely claimed its enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}, whom Nobunaga revered, and this claim remained thereafter. In {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}), it was returned from the Mure settlement to the Nakamura settlement. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|WD=Q18639|1=1939}}), due to river improvement works on the {{ill|lt=Ai River|Q11450512|WD=Q11450512|1=Ai River}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site. In Shōwa 15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}}), it received and repurposed the building of the north-facing Hachiman Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|WD=Q11574477|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}}, which had been built in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|WD=Q6747|1=1606}}) by {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|WD=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}}, as its shrine hall. In Shōwa 54 ({{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|WD=Q2484|1=1979}}), the main hall was restored. === Grounds === File:Mure-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall　　 File:Mure 03.JPG|Worship hall There are no other subsidiary shrines within the grounds. === On-site information === ; Address * Nakamura-chō 5-7, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Hankyu Corporation|Q473058|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu|1=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|WD=Q2053737|lt=Kyoto Main Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|WD=Q3969806|1=Ibaraki-shi Station}} (about a 15-minute walk) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), ''Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District'' (1974). {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, ''Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples'' (1989). {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} * Osaka Prefectural Shinto Youth Association (ed.), ''Directory of Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture'' (1971). {{NCID|BA39399841}} * ''Japanese Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ibaraki City—entries for Nakamura and Mure Village === External links === {{Commonscat|Mure-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/ibaraki-city/m12_21_ibaragi_mure.html Mure Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto navigation}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mure Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mure-jinja}} [[JA:牟禮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11570305}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|茨木市の牟禮神社|飯綱町の牟禮神社|牟礼神社 (飯綱町)}} {{神社 |名称 = Mure Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mure-jinja torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Nakamura-chō 5-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|14.88|N|135|34|51.16|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mure Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mure Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mure Shrine''' (むれじんじゃ; simplified characters: '''牟礼神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (たてはやすさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのこやねのみこと) === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists it as “Mure Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}, Settsu Province,” and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'', in the section on {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, there is the passage “Miko Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of Mure no Wake,” leading to a theory that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおなかつひこのみこと), the ancestor of the Mure clan.However, the relationship between “Mure no Wake” and this locality is not clear (from the “Mure Village” entry in ''Place Names of Osaka Prefecture''). Nevertheless, it is said that in order to avoid the temple and shrine burnings carried out by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine falsely claimed its enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}, whom Nobunaga revered, and this claim remained thereafter. In {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}), it was returned from the Mure settlement to the Nakamura settlement. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|WD=Q18639|1=1939}}), due to river improvement works on the {{ill|lt=Ai River|Q11450512|WD=Q11450512|1=Ai River}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site. In Shōwa 15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}}), it received and repurposed the building of the north-facing Hachiman Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|WD=Q11574477|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}}, which had been built in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|WD=Q6747|1=1606}}) by {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|WD=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}}, as its shrine hall. In Shōwa 54 ({{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|WD=Q2484|1=1979}}), the main hall was restored. === Grounds === File:Mure-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall　　 File:Mure 03.JPG|Worship hall There are no other subsidiary shrines within the grounds. === On-site information === ; Address * Nakamura-chō 5-7, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Hankyu Corporation|Q473058|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu|1=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|WD=Q2053737|lt=Kyoto Main Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|WD=Q3969806|1=Ibaraki-shi Station}} (about a 15-minute walk) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), ''Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District'' (1974). {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, ''Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples'' (1989). {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} * Osaka Prefectural Shinto Youth Association (ed.), ''Directory of Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture'' (1971). {{NCID|BA39399841}} * ''Japanese Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ibaraki City—entries for Nakamura and Mure Village === External links === {{Commonscat|Mure-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/ibaraki-city/m12_21_ibaragi_mure.html Mure Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto navigation}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mure Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * むれじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120905000160 == Commons category (P373) == * Mure-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市中村町5-7', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mure-jinja}} [[JA:牟禮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11570305}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|茨木市の牟禮神社|飯綱町の牟禮神社|牟礼神社 (飯綱町)}} {{神社 |名称 = Mure Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mure-jinja torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Nakamura-chō 5-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|14.88|N|135|34|51.16|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mure Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mure Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mure Shrine''' (むれじんじゃ; simplified characters: '''牟礼神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (たてはやすさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのこやねのみこと) === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists it as “Mure Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}, Settsu Province,” and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'', in the section on {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, there is the passage “Miko Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of Mure no Wake,” leading to a theory that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおなかつひこのみこと), the ancestor of the Mure clan.However, the relationship between “Mure no Wake” and this locality is not clear (from the “Mure Village” entry in ''Place Names of Osaka Prefecture''). Nevertheless, it is said that in order to avoid the temple and shrine burnings carried out by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine falsely claimed its enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}, whom Nobunaga revered, and this claim remained thereafter. In {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}), it was returned from the Mure settlement to the Nakamura settlement. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|WD=Q18639|1=1939}}), due to river improvement works on the {{ill|lt=Ai River|Q11450512|WD=Q11450512|1=Ai River}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site. In Shōwa 15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}}), it received and repurposed the building of the north-facing Hachiman Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|WD=Q11574477|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}}, which had been built in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|WD=Q6747|1=1606}}) by {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|WD=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}}, as its shrine hall. In Shōwa 54 ({{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|WD=Q2484|1=1979}}), the main hall was restored. === Grounds === File:Mure-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall　　 File:Mure 03.JPG|Worship hall There are no other subsidiary shrines within the grounds. === On-site information === ; Address * Nakamura-chō 5-7, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Hankyu Corporation|Q473058|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu|1=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|WD=Q2053737|lt=Kyoto Main Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|WD=Q3969806|1=Ibaraki-shi Station}} (about a 15-minute walk) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), ''Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District'' (1974). {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, ''Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples'' (1989). {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} * Osaka Prefectural Shinto Youth Association (ed.), ''Directory of Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture'' (1971). {{NCID|BA39399841}} * ''Japanese Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ibaraki City—entries for Nakamura and Mure Village === External links === {{Commonscat|Mure-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/ibaraki-city/m12_21_ibaragi_mure.html Mure Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto navigation}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mure Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mure-jinja}} [[JA:牟禮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11570305}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|茨木市の牟禮神社|飯綱町の牟禮神社|牟礼神社 (飯綱町)}} {{神社 |名称 = Mure Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mure-jinja torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Nakamura-chō 5-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|14.88|N|135|34|51.16|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mure Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mure Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mure Shrine''' (むれじんじゃ; simplified characters: '''牟礼神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (たてはやすさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのこやねのみこと) === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists it as “Mure Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}, Settsu Province,” and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'', in the section on {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, there is the passage “Miko Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of Mure no Wake,” leading to a theory that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおなかつひこのみこと), the ancestor of the Mure clan.However, the relationship between “Mure no Wake” and this locality is not clear (from the “Mure Village” entry in ''Place Names of Osaka Prefecture''). Nevertheless, it is said that in order to avoid the temple and shrine burnings carried out by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine falsely claimed its enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}, whom Nobunaga revered, and this claim remained thereafter. In {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}), it was returned from the Mure settlement to the Nakamura settlement. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|WD=Q18639|1=1939}}), due to river improvement works on the {{ill|lt=Ai River|Q11450512|WD=Q11450512|1=Ai River}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site. In Shōwa 15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}}), it received and repurposed the building of the north-facing Hachiman Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|WD=Q11574477|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}}, which had been built in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|WD=Q6747|1=1606}}) by {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|WD=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}}, as its shrine hall. In Shōwa 54 ({{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|WD=Q2484|1=1979}}), the main hall was restored. === Grounds === File:Mure-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall　　 File:Mure 03.JPG|Worship hall There are no other subsidiary shrines within the grounds. === On-site information === ; Address * Nakamura-chō 5-7, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Hankyu Corporation|Q473058|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu|1=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|WD=Q2053737|lt=Kyoto Main Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|WD=Q3969806|1=Ibaraki-shi Station}} (about a 15-minute walk) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), ''Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District'' (1974). {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, ''Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples'' (1989). {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} * Osaka Prefectural Shinto Youth Association (ed.), ''Directory of Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture'' (1971). {{NCID|BA39399841}} * ''Japanese Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ibaraki City—entries for Nakamura and Mure Village === External links === {{Commonscat|Mure-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/ibaraki-city/m12_21_ibaragi_mure.html Mure Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto navigation}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mure Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mure-jinja}} [[JA:牟禮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11570305}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|茨木市の牟禮神社|飯綱町の牟禮神社|牟礼神社 (飯綱町)}} {{神社 |名称 = Mure Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mure-jinja torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Nakamura-chō 5-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|14.88|N|135|34|51.16|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mure Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mure Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mure Shrine''' (むれじんじゃ; simplified characters: '''牟礼神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (たてはやすさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのこやねのみこと) === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists it as “Mure Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}, Settsu Province,” and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'', in the section on {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, there is the passage “Miko Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of Mure no Wake,” leading to a theory that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおなかつひこのみこと), the ancestor of the Mure clan.However, the relationship between “Mure no Wake” and this locality is not clear (from the “Mure Village” entry in ''Place Names of Osaka Prefecture''). Nevertheless, it is said that in order to avoid the temple and shrine burnings carried out by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine falsely claimed its enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}, whom Nobunaga revered, and this claim remained thereafter. In {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}), it was returned from the Mure settlement to the Nakamura settlement. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|WD=Q18639|1=1939}}), due to river improvement works on the {{ill|lt=Ai River|Q11450512|WD=Q11450512|1=Ai River}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site. In Shōwa 15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}}), it received and repurposed the building of the north-facing Hachiman Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|WD=Q11574477|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}}, which had been built in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|WD=Q6747|1=1606}}) by {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|WD=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}}, as its shrine hall. In Shōwa 54 ({{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|WD=Q2484|1=1979}}), the main hall was restored. === Grounds === File:Mure-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall　　 File:Mure 03.JPG|Worship hall There are no other subsidiary shrines within the grounds. === On-site information === ; Address * Nakamura-chō 5-7, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Hankyu Corporation|Q473058|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu|1=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|WD=Q2053737|lt=Kyoto Main Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|WD=Q3969806|1=Ibaraki-shi Station}} (about a 15-minute walk) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), ''Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District'' (1974). {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, ''Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples'' (1989). {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} * Osaka Prefectural Shinto Youth Association (ed.), ''Directory of Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture'' (1971). {{NCID|BA39399841}} * ''Japanese Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ibaraki City—entries for Nakamura and Mure Village === External links === {{Commonscat|Mure-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/ibaraki-city/m12_21_ibaragi_mure.html Mure Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto navigation}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * むれじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120905000160 == Commons category (P373) == * Mure-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市中村町5-7', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mure-jinja}} [[JA:牟禮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11570305}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|茨木市の牟禮神社|飯綱町の牟禮神社|牟礼神社 (飯綱町)}} {{神社 |名称 = Mure Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mure-jinja torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|qid=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Nakamura-chō 5-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|14.88|N|135|34|51.16|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mure Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|qid=Q2922|1=October 14}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mure Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mure Shrine''' (むれじんじゃ; simplified characters: '''牟礼神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|qid=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (たてはやすさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのこやねのみこと) === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists it as “Mure Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|qid=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}, Settsu Province,” and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'', in the section on {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, there is the passage “Miko Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of Mure no Wake,” leading to a theory that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおなかつひこのみこと), the ancestor of the Mure clan.However, the relationship between “Mure no Wake” and this locality is not clear (from the “Mure Village” entry in ''Place Names of Osaka Prefecture''). Nevertheless, it is said that in order to avoid the temple and shrine burnings carried out by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine falsely claimed its enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|qid=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}, whom Nobunaga revered, and this claim remained thereafter. In {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|qid=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|qid=Q6824|1=1704}}), it was returned from the Mure settlement to the Nakamura settlement. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|qid=Q18639|1=1939}}), due to river improvement works on the {{ill|lt=Ai River|Q11450512|qid=Q11450512|1=Ai River}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site. In Shōwa 15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|qid=Q18633|1=1940}}), it received and repurposed the building of the north-facing Hachiman Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|qid=Q11574477|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}}, which had been built in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|qid=Q6747|1=1606}}) by {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|qid=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}}, as its shrine hall. In Shōwa 54 ({{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|qid=Q2484|1=1979}}), the main hall was restored. === Grounds === File:Mure-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall　　 File:Mure 03.JPG|Worship hall There are no other subsidiary shrines within the grounds. === On-site information === ; Address * Nakamura-chō 5-7, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|qid=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Hankyu Corporation|Q473058|qid=Q473058|lt=Hankyu|1=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|qid=Q2053737|lt=Kyoto Main Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|qid=Q3969806|1=Ibaraki-shi Station}} (about a 15-minute walk) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), ''Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District'' (1974). {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, ''Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples'' (1989). {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} * Osaka Prefectural Shinto Youth Association (ed.), ''Directory of Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture'' (1971). {{NCID|BA39399841}} * ''Japanese Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ibaraki City—entries for Nakamura and Mure Village === External links === {{Commonscat|Mure-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/ibaraki-city/m12_21_ibaragi_mure.html Mure Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto navigation}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * むれじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120905000160 == Commons category (P373) == * Mure-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市中村町5-7', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mure-jinja}} [[JA:牟禮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11570305}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|茨木市の牟禮神社|飯綱町の牟禮神社|牟礼神社 (飯綱町)}} {{神社 |名称 = Mure Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mure-jinja torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|qid=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Nakamura-chō 5-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|14.88|N|135|34|51.16|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mure Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|qid=Q2922|1=October 14}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mure Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mure Shrine''' (むれじんじゃ; simplified characters: '''牟礼神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|qid=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (たてはやすさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのこやねのみこと) === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists it as “Mure Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|qid=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}, Settsu Province,” and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'', in the section on {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, there is the passage “Miko Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of Mure no Wake,” leading to a theory that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおなかつひこのみこと), the ancestor of the Mure clan.However, the relationship between “Mure no Wake” and this locality is not clear (from the “Mure Village” entry in ''Place Names of Osaka Prefecture''). Nevertheless, it is said that in order to avoid the temple and shrine burnings carried out by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine falsely claimed its enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|qid=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}, whom Nobunaga revered, and this claim remained thereafter. In {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|qid=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|qid=Q6824|1=1704}}), it was returned from the Mure settlement to the Nakamura settlement. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|qid=Q18639|1=1939}}), due to river improvement works on the {{ill|lt=Ai River|Q11450512|qid=Q11450512|1=Ai River}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site. In Shōwa 15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|qid=Q18633|1=1940}}), it received and repurposed the building of the north-facing Hachiman Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|qid=Q11574477|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}}, which had been built in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|qid=Q6747|1=1606}}) by {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|qid=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}}, as its shrine hall. In Shōwa 54 ({{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|qid=Q2484|1=1979}}), the main hall was restored. === Grounds === File:Mure-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall　　 File:Mure 03.JPG|Worship hall There are no other subsidiary shrines within the grounds. === On-site information === ; Address * Nakamura-chō 5-7, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|qid=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Hankyu Corporation|Q473058|qid=Q473058|lt=Hankyu|1=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|qid=Q2053737|lt=Kyoto Main Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|qid=Q3969806|1=Ibaraki-shi Station}} (about a 15-minute walk) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), ''Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District'' (1974). {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, ''Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples'' (1989). {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} * Osaka Prefectural Shinto Youth Association (ed.), ''Directory of Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture'' (1971). {{NCID|BA39399841}} * ''Japanese Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ibaraki City—entries for Nakamura and Mure Village === External links === {{Commonscat|Mure-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/ibaraki-city/m12_21_ibaragi_mure.html Mure Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto navigation}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mure-jinja}} [[JA:牟禮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11570305}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|茨木市の牟禮神社|飯綱町の牟禮神社|牟礼神社 (飯綱町)}} {{神社 |名称 = Mure Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mure-jinja torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|qid=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Nakamura-chō 5-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|14.88|N|135|34|51.16|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mure Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|qid=Q2922|1=October 14}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mure Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mure Shrine''' (むれじんじゃ; simplified characters: '''牟礼神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|qid=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (たてはやすさのおのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのこやねのみこと) === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists it as “Mure Shrine, {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|qid=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}, Settsu Province,” and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'', in the section on {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, there is the passage “Miko Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto is the ancestral deity of Mure no Wake,” leading to a theory that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおなかつひこのみこと), the ancestor of the Mure clan.However, the relationship between “Mure no Wake” and this locality is not clear (from the “Mure Village” entry in ''Place Names of Osaka Prefecture''). Nevertheless, it is said that in order to avoid the temple and shrine burnings carried out by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine falsely claimed its enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|qid=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}, whom Nobunaga revered, and this claim remained thereafter. In {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|qid=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|qid=Q6824|1=1704}}), it was returned from the Mure settlement to the Nakamura settlement. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|qid=Q18639|1=1939}}), due to river improvement works on the {{ill|lt=Ai River|Q11450512|qid=Q11450512|1=Ai River}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site. In Shōwa 15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|qid=Q18633|1=1940}}), it received and repurposed the building of the north-facing Hachiman Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|Q11574477|qid=Q11574477|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}}, which had been built in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|qid=Q6747|1=1606}}) by {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|qid=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}}, as its shrine hall. In Shōwa 54 ({{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|qid=Q2484|1=1979}}), the main hall was restored. === Grounds === File:Mure-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall　　 File:Mure 03.JPG|Worship hall There are no other subsidiary shrines within the grounds. === On-site information === ; Address * Nakamura-chō 5-7, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|qid=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Hankyu Corporation|Q473058|qid=Q473058|lt=Hankyu|1=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|qid=Q2053737|lt=Kyoto Main Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|qid=Q3969806|1=Ibaraki-shi Station}} (about a 15-minute walk) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), ''Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District'' (1974). {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} * Ibaraki City Board of Education, ''Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples'' (1989). {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} * Osaka Prefectural Shinto Youth Association (ed.), ''Directory of Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture'' (1971). {{NCID|BA39399841}} * ''Japanese Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Osaka Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Ibaraki City—entries for Nakamura and Mure Village === External links === {{Commonscat|Mure-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/ibaraki-city/m12_21_ibaragi_mure.html Mure Shrine] (Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto navigation}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mure Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]]
